title: Adding a drop down option under a column
excerpt: I am setting a sales pipeline. One the columns I want to setup a drop down for each new Entry. For instance, for new client Jon Doe, I want to assign a Industry. Is there a way to select from a list or drop down for Jon …

posts.author: Tabby (Tabish)
posts.cookedHtml: I am setting a sales pipeline. One the columns I want to setup a drop down for each new Entry. For instance, for new client Jon Doe, I want to assign a Industry. Is there a way to select from a list or drop down for Jon Doe under, for instance, an industy column. I work with 10 industries so the list will have 10 names i can choose from.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Perhaps I’m not understanding? All you’d need to do is add a ‘Dropdown’ column type and then list out the options you want to include. Then for each new lead, just pick the industry related to that contact.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Tabby,

You can always use a Status column or Dropdown column for this. Keep in mind that with the status column you can only select one option (industry). However, with the Dropdown column, you will be able to select multiple industries if needed.
